title: liriopogon biflorum
intro: Liriopogon biflorum, also known as the two-flowered grass-lily, is a flowering plant species native to Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Liriopogon biflorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Liriopogon biflorum can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
